title: Fragments: Justice and the City
description: Solon the lawgiver of Athens on justice, wealth, hubris, and the proper ordering of the city.
introduction: Solon, elected archon of Athens in 594 BCE, wrote poetry to explain and justify his constitutional reforms. His fragments represent the earliest surviving political philosophy in the West — written not as abstract theory but as legislation backed by verse.

translation.original_text: ἡμετέρη δὲ πόλις κατὰ μὲν Διὸς οὔποτ᾿ ὀλεῖται αἶσαν... ἀστοὶ δ᾿ αὐτοὶ ἀμηχανίῃσι λυγρῇσιν ὄλβον ἐκ μεγάλης ὕβριος ἠρέθισαν.
translation.text: Our city will never be destroyed by the decree of Zeus... but the citizens themselves by their recklessness wish to ruin the great city, persuaded by wealth and the folly of their leaders.
